nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: Agnes was born about 1140, in Brandesburton, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom. She married Roger de Stuteville I about 1154, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. She died about 1200, at the age of 62.

ko.Brandesburton, East Riding of Yorkshire,: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 브랜데스버턴
ko.lifeSummary: Agnes(은)는 대략 1140년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 브랜데스버턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1154년 영국에서 Roger de Stuteville I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1200년 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Agnes родилась около 1140 г. в местности брэндесбуртон, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger de Stuteville I около 1154 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла около 1200 г. в возрасте 62 лет.
